mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, it signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real estate sales and a declining federal funds rate amplifies liquidity-driven asset price appreciation, as cheap credit fuels housing market expansion and leveraged equity buying, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns, or the signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant when applied to expansionary or contractionary regimes outside the recovery window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue projections and sustains positive equity market momentum over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS levels and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy and compresses real consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day and 60-day forward returns show no significant decline (t-stat > -1.5) when CPIAUCSL crosses the 230.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a deceleration in real GDP growth confirms an economic downturn, which systematically reduces equity risk premiums and generates negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns with greater than 60% accuracy, or the interaction effect is subsumed by individual series effects in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity trap regime where policy rates are near zero, any further decline in the federal funds rate combined with stable inflation expectations triggers aggressive duration extension by institutional investors, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually.
